jokes.text: I can still taste the sauce. And get this: It's all-you-can-eat! This is like some beautiful dream!
jokes.setup: Barney describes a new BBQ restaurant to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's over-the-top reaction to all-you-can-eat BBQ as a 'beautiful dream'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer reaction - his childlike wonder at simple pleasures is perfectly in character and relatable

jokes.text: Marge, I've got five words to say to you. Greasy Joe's Bottomless BBQ Pit.
jokes.setup: Homer dramatically announces he has five words to say
jokes.punchline: The restaurant name is actually seven words, not five
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his inability to count combined with dramatic presentation is both character-specific and structurally sound

jokes.text: You promised you'd only have six servings of pork a week.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to go to an all-you-can-eat BBQ place
jokes.punchline: The revelation that Homer has a 'limit' of six servings of pork per week
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - the absurdity that six servings of pork per week is considered moderation in the Simpson household

jokes.text: I'm only human.
jokes.setup: Marge reminds him of his pork limit
jokes.punchline: Homer's weak excuse for his gluttony
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Homer's pathetic justification for his excess

jokes.text: Hey, alley cats. Save it for the honeymoon!
jokes.setup: Wedding guests being affectionate
jokes.punchline: Patty's crude interruption using outdated slang
jokes.explanation: Classic Patty - cynical, inappropriate, using dated expressions that reveal her personality

jokes.text: F. L. No, I'm sorry. That's a 'C,' isn't it?
jokes.setup: Homer taking what appears to be an eye test
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete inability to identify basic letters
jokes.explanation: Homer's stupidity played for comedy - his confident incorrectness is endearing and character-specific

jokes.text: If that was a car, you'd be dead now.
jokes.setup: Homer failing his eye test badly
jokes.punchline: The DMV employee's matter-of-fact observation about the deadly consequences
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - the casual delivery of a morbid observation with perfect economy

jokes.text: Hello, Selma. Hello, Stanley.
jokes.setup: Groom reminisces about meeting his bride Martha
jokes.punchline: He actually says 'Hello, Selma' instead of Martha's name
jokes.explanation: Excellent structural comedy - the romantic flashback is undercut by him getting the wrong sister's name

jokes.text: Is this seat taken? Yes! Hey, beat it!
jokes.setup: Homer politely asking about a seat
jokes.punchline: Immediate aggressive rejection
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective contrast between Homer's politeness and the rude response

jokes.text: Poor Brandy.
jokes.setup: The groom talking about how lucky he was to meet his bride
jokes.punchline: Lisa's innocent sympathy for whatever woman the groom might have ended up with instead
jokes.explanation: Brilliant child logic - Lisa's innocent misunderstanding creates a perfectly timed comedic observation

jokes.text: I'm sure you'd resent my pity, so I'll hope you're one of the statistically small number of single 40-year-olds who find their fair prince.
jokes.setup: Selma wondering if she'll ever get married
jokes.punchline: Lisa's brutally honest statistical analysis delivered with fake optimism
jokes.explanation: Classic Lisa - precocious intelligence delivering harsh truths with mathematical precision and fake kindness

jokes.text: I'll tell you one thing, Greasy Joe is sorry he ever met me!
jokes.setup: Homer returns from the all-you-can-eat restaurant
jokes.punchline: His proud declaration that he defeated the restaurant
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer victory lap - his pride in eating so much he became a liability to the business

jokes.text: No, that's just my sister-in-law.
jokes.setup: Homer apparently responding to someone's shocked reaction to Selma
jokes.punchline: Homer's matter-of-fact clarification that deflates any drama
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff - we can infer someone's horrified reaction to Selma's appearance from Homer's casual response

jokes.text: I'll get right to it. I'm getting older and uglier. Please, Marge, help me find a man before it's too late.
jokes.setup: Selma decides to be direct with Marge
jokes.punchline: Her brutally honest self-assessment
jokes.explanation: Selma's self-deprecating honesty is both funny and genuinely sad

jokes.text: Wait, which one's Selma?
jokes.setup: Homer being asked to find Selma a husband
jokes.punchline: Homer can't even tell his in-laws apart
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his obliviousness to his own family members is both realistic and funny

jokes.text: Patty chose celibacy but Selma simply had it thrust upon her.
jokes.setup: Homer confusing the twins' personalities
jokes.punchline: Marge's distinction between voluntary and involuntary singleness with clever wordplay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay - 'thrust upon her' works both literally and as innuendo, plus it's a clever character distinction

jokes.text: Why should she have a better husband than you?
jokes.setup: Marge listing qualities Selma's ideal man should have
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent self-burn
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer - his complete lack of self-awareness creates an accidental insult to himself while defending Marge

jokes.text: Excellent.
jokes.setup: Learning that sodium tetrasulfate kills grass
jokes.punchline: Bart's enthusiastic approval of the destructive chemical
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart - his delight in anything destructive, delivered with Mr. Burns' signature word

jokes.text: Vandalism fans, hold on to your hats. He'll never get away with it. He just goes too far.
jokes.setup: Students discuss Bart's upcoming vandalism
jokes.punchline: They're both excited and concerned about Bart's extremism
jokes.explanation: Establishes Bart's reputation for extreme pranks among his peers

jokes.text: The sheer contempt makes me wish I could use the board of education.
jokes.setup: Skinner is frustrated with Bart's behavior
jokes.punchline: His pun on 'board of education' (school board vs. wooden paddle)
jokes.explanation: Classic pun on corporal punishment terminology, fits Skinner's character

jokes.text: The sheer contempt makes me wish I could use the board of education.
jokes.setup: Skinner frustrated with Bart's vandalism
jokes.punchline: Euphemism for corporal punishment delivered with Skinner's pompous vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner moment - his formal language and nostalgic references to old-school discipline

jokes.text: Homer Sexual. Wait one second, let me check. Homer Sexual! Come on, one of you guys has got to be Homer Sexual!
jokes.setup: Bart prank calling asking for Homer Sexual
jokes.punchline: Moe loudly asking the bar for someone with that name
jokes.explanation: Classic prank call joke - the delayed realization and Moe's earnest announcement to the bar elevates it

jokes.text: Don't look at me!
jokes.setup: Moe asking if anyone is 'Homer Sexual'
jokes.punchline: Barney's defensive response
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up - Barney's defensive reaction implies everyone's looking at him suspiciously

jokes.text: You little punk! If I get ahold of you... I'll rip your face off! You'll do what, young man?
jokes.setup: Moe threatens Bart over the phone
jokes.punchline: Skinner's authoritative interruption thinking Moe is a student
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection as Skinner mistakes Moe's threat for student behavior

jokes.text: Sorry, Principal Skinner. It's a bad connection, I think.
jokes.setup: Skinner catches Moe making threats
jokes.punchline: Bart's innocent excuse about the connection
jokes.explanation: Bart's quick thinking to cover for the prank call situation

jokes.text: Only to my job. But if you weren't, you'd tend to go for a girl?
jokes.setup: Skinner says he's married to his job
jokes.punchline: Homer's awkward way of asking about sexual orientation
jokes.explanation: Homer's clumsy attempt at determining Skinner's romantic preferences

jokes.text: These pants come off at night just like everybody else's.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Skinner would go for a girl
jokes.punchline: Skinner's awkward confirmation of his heterosexuality
jokes.explanation: Perfectly awkward Skinner - his formal, uncomfortable way of expressing basic human normalcy

jokes.text: This dinner wouldn't be a plan to set me up with some... unmarried relation, would it? Because I can--
jokes.setup: Skinner arrives at dinner
jokes.punchline: His immediate suspicion of a setup
jokes.explanation: Skinner's wariness about social situations and potential romantic setups

jokes.text: Be still, my foolish heart.
jokes.setup: Skinner about to meet Homer's sister-in-law
jokes.punchline: Skinner's overly romantic, literary reaction
jokes.explanation: Good Skinner characterization - his pretentious, romantic language for a simple situation

jokes.text: Here we go. Boy meets beast.
jokes.setup: Setting up Skinner to meet Selma
jokes.punchline: Homer's twist on 'boy meets girl' revealing his view of Selma
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with character insight - Homer's casual cruelty toward Selma in a familiar phrase

jokes.text: Hey, tubs, I'm Patty!
jokes.setup: Skinner expecting to meet the lovely Selma
jokes.punchline: Meeting the wrong, hostile sister who immediately insults him
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty - aggressive, insulting, and the wrong sister, destroying Skinner's romantic expectations

jokes.text: D'oh! Wrong one.
jokes.setup: Skinner meeting Patty instead of Selma
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature exclamation at his mistake
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase used perfectly for his typical mistake-making

jokes.text: Nothing more to tell, really. The Nile smells and the horseflies are huge.
jokes.setup: Skinner asks about Patty's trip to Egypt
jokes.punchline: Her unromantic, practical assessment of the exotic destination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty - her ability to make even exotic travel sound mundane and unpleasant

jokes.text: Marvelous. Just marvelous.
jokes.setup: Patty describing Egypt negatively
jokes.punchline: Skinner's polite but obviously forced enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Good awkward moment - Skinner's desperate attempt to find common ground despite obvious incompatibility

jokes.text: Marvelous. Just marvelous. Well, Selma hated Egypt too. A camel spit on her.
jokes.setup: Skinner tries to be polite about Patty's Egypt story
jokes.punchline: Homer's addition about Selma's even worse experience
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both sisters had terrible experiences with travel

jokes.text: Your first date in 25 years is more important than playing cards.
jokes.setup: Patty mentions existing plans
jokes.punchline: Selma's revelation about the extreme length of Patty's dating drought
jokes.explanation: Devastating revelation about Patty's romantic history - both funny and sad

jokes.text: Here you go. Smoke them in good health.
jokes.setup: Selling cigarettes
jokes.punchline: The contradiction of wishing health while selling cigarettes
jokes.explanation: Smart observational comedy - the inherent contradiction in cigarette sales with perfect Apu politeness

jokes.text: You haven't seen the last of Willie!
jokes.setup: Willie being left with Bart
jokes.punchline: Willie's dramatic, villain-like threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie - his over-dramatic, almost cinematic way of expressing simple frustration

jokes.text: You don't want to show up for your big date looking like Yosemite Sam.
jokes.setup: Selma helps Patty prepare for her date
jokes.punchline: Her comparison of Patty to the disheveled cartoon character
jokes.explanation: Good visual comparison that's both insulting and helpful sisterly advice

jokes.text: You don't want to show up for your big date looking like Yosemite Sam.
jokes.setup: Preparing Patty for her date
jokes.punchline: Comparing Patty's appearance to the cartoon character
jokes.explanation: Good visual reference - the specific cartoon character creates a clear, funny mental image

jokes.text: Achingly beautiful.
jokes.setup: Patty asking how she looks after grooming
jokes.punchline: Selma's obviously false flattery
jokes.explanation: Good sister dynamic - the over-the-top compliment that's clearly sarcastic

jokes.text: Food tastes better when you're revolving.
jokes.setup: At a revolving restaurant
jokes.punchline: Skinner's earnest belief in this absurd concept
jokes.explanation: Good absurd observation - the kind of thing someone might actually say about a gimmicky restaurant

jokes.text: Little Jimmy Pearson. Class of '71, right? Get this woman a glass of water! And tuck in your shirt. Nearly 30 and still working as a busboy! Standardized testing never lies.
jokes.setup: Recognizing a former student
jokes.punchline: Skinner's authoritarian behavior and condescending evaluation
jokes.explanation: Excellent Skinner moment - can't turn off his principal mode, complete with educational philosophy and authority

jokes.text: Two minutes younger. Skin like a china doll. Bosoms till Tuesday.
jokes.setup: Comparing herself to Patty
jokes.punchline: Selma's bitter, specific jealousy with the odd 'till Tuesday' expression
jokes.explanation: Good sibling rivalry - specific physical comparisons with Selma's unique way of expressing size

jokes.text: Oh, yes. Plenty of fish. We just don't have any bait.
jokes.setup: Marge saying there are plenty of fish in the sea
jokes.punchline: Homer extending the metaphor to reveal their lack of attractive qualities
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor extension - Homer accidentally makes a brutally honest assessment while trying to be helpful

jokes.text: I can't forget what happened to that poor dingo. What could've done it? It was probably just a wallaby. Now come on.
jokes.setup: They're watching what appears to be an Australian nature horror film
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous suggestion that a wallaby could kill a dingo
jokes.explanation: Absurd B-movie dialogue playing on Australian animal stereotypes

jokes.text: Oh, that was the worst movie I've ever seen. Not as bad as the service at the revolving restaurant.
jokes.setup: Leaving what appears to be a movie
jokes.punchline: Both hating the movie but also bonding over shared complaints
jokes.explanation: Good character development - showing how their shared negativity actually creates compatibility

jokes.text: Isn't it nice we hate the same things?
jokes.setup: Both complaining about their experiences
jokes.punchline: Skinner's romantic observation about their shared negativity
jokes.explanation: Brilliant relationship comedy - finding romance in shared misanthropy is both realistic and funny

jokes.text: Thursday, I'm going food shopping. You can come if you like. Until then.
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to make more romantic plans
jokes.punchline: Patty's utterly unromantic counter-offer
jokes.explanation: Perfect Patty - completely practical and unromantic, yet Skinner finds it charming

jokes.text: Oh, I wish I could make a P.A. announcement to the whole world. Attention! Seymour Skinner is in love!
jokes.setup: Skinner being in love
jokes.punchline: His desire to announce it like school business over the PA system
jokes.explanation: Excellent character integration - Skinner can only express romance through his principal persona and school systems

jokes.text: Oh, I wish I could make a P.A. announcement to the whole world. Attention! Seymour Skinner is in love!
jokes.setup: Skinner is smitten after the kiss
jokes.punchline: His desire to broadcast his feelings through school P.A. system
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Skinner's romantic expression filtered through his principal identity

jokes.text: That's cool. Skinner's gonna kill you! Skinner? He works for me now.
jokes.setup: Student warning Bart about Skinner
jokes.punchline: Bart's confident assertion of having power over the principal
jokes.explanation: Good power dynamic comedy - Bart's recognition that he has leverage through the family connection

jokes.text: Patty. I hate to pull you away from your exercise... but I wanted you to be first to know. I plan to ask your aunt to marry me.
jokes.setup: Skinner interrupts Bart's punishment to share news
jokes.punchline: His formal announcement of marriage plans to a 10-year-old
jokes.explanation: Skinner's inappropriate sharing of adult romantic plans with a student

jokes.text: Your funeral.
jokes.setup: Skinner announcing his intention to propose
jokes.punchline: Bart's ominous response to the marriage news
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor - Bart's casual prediction of doom for Skinner's romantic plans

jokes.text: Like my wife's ugly sister. Wheel her in, Homer. I'm not picky.
jokes.setup: Homer describing what Selma looks like
jokes.punchline: Barney's enthusiasm despite the description and 'wheel her in' imagery
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Barney's complete lack of standards and the 'wheel her in' visual

jokes.text: My sister's not going out with Barney Gumble! She's no prize pig herself, you know!
jokes.setup: Barney showing interest in Selma
jokes.punchline: Marge's indignant response that accidentally insults her sister
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Marge's defense accidentally becomes an insult to both parties

jokes.text: Can the sweet talk! You're right. It's time to end my girlish dreams... and grab hold of the next train out of the station.
jokes.setup: Selma realizes her romantic situation is hopeless
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic metaphor about settling for whoever's available
jokes.explanation: Selma's resignation expressed through transportation metaphor

jokes.text: Is that Selma? Ring-a-ding-ding!
jokes.setup: Selma arriving all dressed up
jokes.punchline: Barney's enthusiastic vintage expression of approval
jokes.explanation: Decent character moment - Barney's old-fashioned expression of attraction

jokes.text: It takes a ripe piece of cheese to catch the mouse.
jokes.setup: Explaining her dressed-up appearance
jokes.punchline: Selma's self-deprecating metaphor comparing herself to aged cheese
jokes.explanation: Brilliant metaphor - self-aware and funny, perfectly capturing Selma's realistic self-assessment

jokes.text: It's time to give away my love like cheap wine.
jokes.setup: Continuing her philosophy about dating
jokes.punchline: Another self-deprecating metaphor about dispensing love cheaply
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up metaphor - maintains the theme while showing Selma's desperation and humor

jokes.text: Jeezum Crow! Look at the size of that rock! The second most precious jewel here.
jokes.setup: Seeing Skinner's engagement ring
jokes.punchline: Skinner's corny compliment calling Patty the most precious jewel
jokes.explanation: Standard romantic comedy moment - corny but in character for Skinner's formal romantic style

jokes.text: It's not that I don't love you. You love me! Yes. Yes, but--
jokes.setup: Patty responds to the proposal
jokes.punchline: Skinner's excited interruption at the word 'love'
jokes.explanation: Awkward romantic moment with Skinner latching onto the love admission

jokes.text: It's kind of a Catch-22.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she can't marry Skinner
jokes.punchline: Patty's casual use of a literary reference for their relationship problem
jokes.explanation: Good literary reference - unexpected from Patty but perfectly describes their impossible situation

jokes.text: Oh, Springfield Elementary, I will have you back again! After all, tomorrow is another school day!
jokes.setup: Skinner walking away from the house
jokes.punchline: His dramatic declaration parodying 'Gone with the Wind' but about school
jokes.explanation: Excellent Skinner moment - literary parody combined with his obsession with school, perfectly melodramatic

jokes.text: Oh, Springfield Elementary, I will have you back again! After all, tomorrow is another school day!
jokes.setup: Skinner processes his romantic loss
jokes.punchline: His consolation comes from school rather than romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner - finding comfort in his institutional role rather than personal relationships

jokes.text: We decided we loved each other enough never to see each other again.
jokes.setup: Explaining what happened with Skinner
jokes.punchline: The paradoxical logic of their romantic sacrifice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant paradox - the beautiful contradiction of loving someone so much you sacrifice being together

jokes.text: I told you you'd be back!
jokes.setup: Willie confronting someone (likely Bart) who has returned
jokes.punchline: Willie's vindicated prediction coming true
jokes.explanation: Good callback - Willie getting his moment of vindication with his characteristic dramatic flair
